meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Solving No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between theory and execution. Learn to master project management and build your first SaaS app using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Transform your technical anxiety into a structured roadmap. By treating your SaaS app as a series of modular milestones rather than a monolith, you overcome the 'no knowledge' barrier. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, ensuring every line of code serves a specific user objective.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the most intimidating sight for any student. You have the ambition to build a SaaS, but the 'no SaaS knowledge' wall feels like a fortress. You see successful products and assume they were birthed fully formed, missing the messy, iterative reality of professional software development. 

It is time to stop guessing and start engineering. By adopting a professional project management framework, you turn that wall into a staircase. This tutorial is your blueprint to move from a student with an idea to a developer with a live, functional, and scalable SaaS product.

steps.title: Defining the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop trying to build a 'platform' and start building a 'utility'. Define the one core task your SaaS performs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me distill my SaaS idea into a single, high-value MVP feature that can be built in under 48 hours. Provide a feature list and a basic database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP scope and core user stories defined in the Antigravity session.'

steps.title: Scaffolding the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, production-ready directory structure so you aren't guessing where files go.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS boilerplate directory structure using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include clear separation of concerns for API routes and UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure based on the Antigravity output and ensure all folders are correctly linked.'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Don't build it from scratch; implement a managed auth flow early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a secure, best-practice implementation guide for user authentication using Supabase Auth in a Next.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Supabase Auth and create a protected dashboard route that redirects unauthenticated users.'

steps.title: Building the Core Feature
steps.description: Focus on the 'Value-Add' logic. This is where the actual problem-solving happens.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for [Insert Feature Name]. Ensure the code is modular, well-commented, and handles edge cases gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the core business logic into the dashboard and create a UI component to handle user input.'

steps.title: Refining and Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a code review and identify potential bottlenecks before you deploy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior QA Engineer. Review the codebase for common security vulnerabilities and performance issues in a SaaS context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform a comprehensive code review, fix all linting errors, and optimize the database queries for speed.'

pro_tips: Never hardcode your secrets; always use .env files and strictly ignore them in your .gitignore.
Build for mobile-first; 70% of your initial traffic will likely come from mobile devices.
Ship your MVP before you feel ready; perfection is the enemy of the launch cycle.
Use semantic commit messages to track your progress; it makes debugging significantly faster.
Don't optimize for scale until you have your first 10 paying users; keep your infrastructure lean.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to Vercel, share your project link in our Discord community for feedback, and start tracking your user metrics.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build anything. Stop waiting for the perfect moment or the 'right' amount of knowledge. The best way to learn is to build, break, and rebuild. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
